environment.libradtranSource: conda-forge rubin-libradtran (real build of the upstream libRadtran 2.0.6 tarball)

checks.detail: sim 10^2.12 cd/m2 vs anchor 10^2.30 ± 0.45 dex (Koomen 1952 / Rozenberg 1966 zenith luminance at sunset ~1e2..4e2 cd/m2)
sim 10^0.70 cd/m2 vs anchor 10^0.78 ± 0.55 dex (interpolated Koomen/Rozenberg zenith decay (~1 dex per ~2 deg))
sim 10^-1.25 cd/m2 vs anchor 10^-1.22 ± 0.65 dex (interpolated Koomen/Rozenberg zenith decay toward ~1e-3 cd/m2 at ~11 deg)

checks.check: zenith decay slope 4-8 deg vs Patat 2006 (~1 mag/deg, 6-12 deg)
checks.detail: sim 1.22 mag/arcsec2/deg vs literature 0.8-1.2 (ranges differ: 4-8 vs 6-12 deg — tolerance widened ±0.25)

checks.check: all resolved radiances finite and positive
brightness declines monotonically with depression (12 geometries)
sunward brighter than antisolar at alt 10

checks.detail: monotone growth expected as shadow rises antisolar-first

checks.check: AOD response finite and documented (dep 4, alt 10, sunward)

diagnostics.detail: sim 919 lx (10^2.96) vs anchor 10^2.74 ± 0.3 (sunset horizontal illuminance ~400-700 lx)
sim 37.8 lx (10^1.58) vs anchor 10^1.08 ± 0.45 (interpolated twilight illuminance curve)
sim 0.618 lx (10^-0.21) vs anchor 10^-0.46 ± 0.55 (interpolated twilight illuminance curve)

gates.gate: solver+geometry valid in twilight (from solver-validation.json + full core-cell coverage)
gates.evidence: solver-validation pass=True, MYSTIC 7/7 valid, DISORT-invalid-below-horizon=True; core cells 36/36 resolved (expected >=36)

gates.gate: numerical stability adequate for a LUT (coverage + p95 + max)
gates.evidence: resolved coverage 100.0%, p95 rel uncertainty 5.50%, max 37.1%

gates.gate: Monte Carlo uncertainty quantified (repeat groups + spread + sigma-ratio band)
gates.evidence: 2 repeat groups across 2 depressions; empirical/reported ratios [0.83, 0.69] in [0.3,3.0]

gates.gate: runtime practical for the expanded grid
gates.evidence: projected canonical grid (540 unique nodes, 925 runs): 18.5 CPU-h single-core (4.62 h at 4 jobs)

gates.gate: output convertible to the calculator's quantities (p95 grid consistency)
gates.evidence: spectral radiance -> photopic luminance -> nL -> KS-convention mag/arcsec2; fine-vs-node grid consistency p95 0.46%

gates.gate: consistent with broad literature plausibility ranges (NOT primary matched-geometry validation)
gates.evidence: 9/9 broad-anchor+sanity checks passed under UNMATCHED atmospheric assumptions (offline literature anchors). Primary matched-geometry validation is a separate Milestone 3 deliverable.

statusModel.solverFeasibilityStatus: PASS — MYSTIC 1D-spherical backward valid 0-8 deg across core geometry; DISORT/pseudospherical invalid below the horizon
statusModel.numericalStabilityStatus: PASS — median photopic uncertainty 1.14% on resolved cases
statusModel.literaturePlausibilityStatus: PASS (broad, unmatched-atmosphere anchors only) — consistent with Koomen/Rozenberg/Patat plausibility ranges; NOT primary matched-geometry validation
statusModel.primaryObservationalValidationStatus: PENDING (Milestone 3) — matched-geometry Patat/Koomen comparison via synthetic Johnson V
statusModel.firstVisibilityValidationStatus: BLOCKED — no first-visibility observational dataset exists in the repository

demonstratedCore.note: 0-8 deg feasibility demonstrated across the core altitude/azimuth geometry at baseline AOD; AOD extremes sampled only in limited geometries (not validated across the full domain)

provisionalExtension.evidence: ZENITH-ONLY probes resolved to ~9-13% at 4e7 photons; NOT established at all altitudes/azimuths/AOD

notSupported.atmosphere: clouds, refraction (not modelled), aerosol families other than the one Shettle rural profile

supportedDomain.reconciliationNote: This evidence-based domain supersedes any earlier '0-10 deg fully supported' wording in README/SCIENTIFIC_ASSUMPTIONS/FEASIBILITY_REPORT; the full 0-10 deg x 10-90 deg x AOD hypercube is NOT declared supported until Milestone 3 pre-grid worst-geometry cases pass.
